name: Owen v. City of Independence, Missouri
summary: A police chief was dismissed without notice or a hearing after the city manager released incriminating investigative reports to the city council. The municipality argued it should benefit from the same good-faith immunity available to individual officers. The Court held that unlike individual officials, municipalities have no good-faith immunity defense in § 1983 suits.
holding: A municipality may not assert a good-faith or qualified immunity defense in a § 1983 action. The purposes behind the Civil Rights Act — compensating victims of constitutional violations and deterring unconstitutional governmental conduct — are best served by imposing full liability on municipalities when their official policies or customs cause constitutional harm, regardless of whether municipal officers acted in subjective good faith.

legal_definitions.definition: The rule that municipalities and local government units have no good-faith or qualified immunity defense available in § 1983 suits for constitutional violations caused by their official policies or customs. Unlike individual officers, municipalities bear strict liability when an official policy is the moving force behind a constitutional deprivation.
legal_definitions.context: Owen held that § 1983's twin goals of deterrence and compensation require full municipal accountability, without the safe harbor of good faith that individual officers enjoy through qualified immunity.

relevance_to_audit: Eliminates good-faith defenses for municipalities in § 1983 actions; relevant to auditing local government surveillance programs and procurement practices that cause constitutional harm — the city cannot escape liability by claiming its officials believed their policies were lawful.

key_quotes.text: We hold that municipalities and other local governmental units may no longer be shielded from suit by the good faith of their officers or agents.
key_quotes.context: Rejecting the municipal good-faith immunity defense and imposing full liability where an official policy causes a constitutional violation.

procedural_posture: Eighth Circuit affirmed dismissal; reversed and remanded
subsequent_treatment: Good law; municipalities may not plead good faith or qualified immunity in § 1983 actions — confirmed in later municipal liability cases
